response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

During the promotional week, the customer will pay 15% less for each item. The handcrafted table originally priced at $200 will cost $200 - (15% of $200) = $200 - $30 = $170. The set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150 will cost $150 - (15% of $150) = $150 - $22.50 = $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items during the promotional week is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. Reach for the discount, and make sure to apply it to each item separately. ***

The customer pays $297.50 for the handcrafted table and the set of factory-made chairs during the promotional week. The discount is applied to each item individually, ensuring that the customer receives a reduced price on both items. The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers, which means the customer saves 15% on each item they purchase. ***
